featureName: Fix Storage RLS auth.uid() vs users.id Mismatch + Revert Service Role Bypasses
description: The 'documents' and 'email-attachments' bucket RLS policies (migrations 20260220150000 and 20260225100000) use tm.user_id = auth.uid() directly. But team_members.user_id stores users.id (database PK), NOT auth.uid() (Supabase Auth UUID). These are different UUIDs. The legacy 'intervention-documents' bucket policies correctly use get_current_user_id(). Fix: replace auth.uid() with get_my_profile_ids() in all affected policies, then revert the service role bypasses that were added as a workaround.

userStories.title: Fix 'documents' bucket RLS policies — auth.uid() → get_my_profile_ids()
userStories.description: As any user (gestionnaire, locataire, prestataire), when I upload/view/delete documents in the 'documents' bucket, the RLS policies correctly identify me through the users table bridge instead of comparing auth.uid() directly to team_members.user_id.
acceptanceCriteria: INSERT policy uses tm.user_id IN (SELECT get_my_profile_ids()) instead of tm.user_id = auth.uid()
SELECT policy uses tm.user_id IN (SELECT get_my_profile_ids()) instead of tm.user_id = auth.uid()
DELETE policy uses tm.user_id IN (SELECT get_my_profile_ids()) instead of tm.user_id = auth.uid()
userStories.notes: Fixed in migration 20260226110000. Replaced tm.user_id = auth.uid() with tm.user_id IN (SELECT get_my_profile_ids()) in INSERT, SELECT, DELETE policies. Kept documents_update unchanged (uses owner_id = auth.uid()::text which is correct for storage.objects).

userStories.title: Fix 'email-attachments' bucket RLS policies — auth.uid() → get_my_profile_ids()
userStories.description: As a user processing or viewing email attachments, the RLS policies correctly identify me through the users table bridge.
acceptanceCriteria: INSERT policy uses tm.user_id IN (SELECT get_my_profile_ids()) instead of tm.user_id = auth.uid()
SELECT policy uses tm.user_id IN (SELECT get_my_profile_ids()) instead of tm.user_id = auth.uid()
DELETE policy uses tm.user_id IN (SELECT get_my_profile_ids()) instead of tm.user_id = auth.uid()
userStories.notes: Fixed in same migration 20260226110000. Same pattern as documents bucket — replaced auth.uid() with get_my_profile_ids().

userStories.title: Revert service role bypasses in upload routes
userStories.description: As a security-conscious developer, the 3 upload routes should use the authenticated client for storage operations now that RLS is fixed, instead of bypassing RLS with service role.
acceptanceCriteria: create-intervention/route.ts uses authenticated supabase client for storage upload (not service role)
upload-intervention-document/route.ts uses authenticated supabase client for storage upload (not service role)
conversations/upload/route.ts uses authenticated supabase client for storage upload (not service role)
Cleanup operations still use service role or authenticated client consistently
userStories.notes: Reverted all 3 routes to use authenticated supabase client. Removed createServiceRoleSupabaseClient import from upload-intervention-document and conversations/upload. create-intervention keeps import (still used for conversation threads). Added owner_id = auth.uid()::text to DELETE policies so uploaders can clean up their own files on error.

userStories.title: Update MEMORY.md — correct team_members.role documentation
userStories.description: Fix the incorrect assumption in MEMORY.md that team_members.role only has gestionnaire/admin. It actually includes all roles: admin, gestionnaire, locataire, prestataire, proprietaire.
acceptanceCriteria: MEMORY.md correctly documents that team_member_role enum includes all 5 roles
RLS source of truth entry is accurate
New learning about auth.uid() vs users.id in storage RLS is documented
userStories.notes: Corrected 'Two role systems' entry — team_members.role has ALL 5 roles. Added new 'Storage RLS: auth.uid() vs users.id' learning. Updated E2E entry to reflect that service role bypass is no longer needed.
